title: MiniTurtleBot
summary: An ESP32-based mini robotics platform for experimenting with encoder-aware motion control, line following, odometry, and networked robot control on a small TurtleBot-style chassis. This is an iteration of the KSU ATR Lab's research project.
impact: Turned a small custom robot into a flexible robotics testbed by combining hardware bring-up, web-tunable motion behaviors, and Python logging tools for repeatable movement experiments.
details: Built out the base setup layer for motors, encoders, IR sensors, OLED, servo, IMU, and LiDAR so new behaviors could be tested without redoing low-level hardware setup each time.
Implemented movement experiments ranging from straight-line calibration and square-path driving to line following with PID-style steering, onboard pose tracking, and a lightweight Wi-Fi tuning panel.
Added Python tooling for UDP keyboard control and off-board odometry logging, making it easier to drive the robot, capture encoder telemetry, and plot trajectories for path and letter-drawing tests.
stack: IR, encoder, OLED, and LiDAR integration
